tierDefinitions.runtime_projection: Meta_Kim has a formal repo projection, runtime profile, projection layout, sync manifest entry, and validation coverage.
tierDefinitions.dependency_install_target: A dependency installer can target the product, but Meta_Kim does not own a formal runtime projection for it.
tierDefinitions.candidate_probe: External docs or user demand suggest compatibility, but Meta_Kim needs a probe, adapter design, and validation before promotion.

requiredForRuntimeProjection: official docs or local runtime probe evidence

products.label: Claude Code

evidence.claim: Claude is a supported and default sync target.
Claude has a runtime profile and projection layout.

products.decision: Keep as a formal Meta_Kim runtime projection.
products.nextAction: Maintain by changing canonical sources and running meta:sync plus meta:check.

products.label: Codex

evidence.claim: Codex is a supported and default sync target.
Codex has a runtime profile and projection layout.

products.decision: Keep as a formal Meta_Kim runtime projection.
products.nextAction: Preserve .agents/skills as the project skill root and keep Codex hook validation current.

products.label: OpenClaw

evidence.claim: OpenClaw is a supported and default sync target.
OpenClaw is a compatible formal projection; stricter tool-denial behavior must be backed by OpenClaw-side strict tests, and can merge only after the evidence passes review.
OpenClaw workspace bootstrap injects AGENTS.md, SOUL.md, and BOOTSTRAP.md from the active workspace.
OpenClaw internal hooks cover command/lifecycle automation; blocking/canceling policy belongs to typed plugin hooks.

products.decision: Keep as a compatible formal Meta_Kim projection with OpenClaw-native workspace, skill, config, and hook surfaces.
products.nextAction: Community PRs that improve OpenClaw behavior are welcome; contributors must include strict OpenClaw self-test evidence, especially for typed plugin tool-denial behavior, and changes can merge only after the evidence passes review.

products.label: Cursor

evidence.claim: Cursor is a supported and default sync target.
Cursor has a runtime profile and projection layout.
Cursor project subagents are markdown files under .cursor/agents with YAML frontmatter.
Cursor hooks include preToolUse/postToolUse and failClosed for security-critical hooks.
Cursor project rules use .cursor/rules .mdc files and AGENTS.md guidance.

products.decision: Keep as a formal Meta_Kim runtime projection with official subagent/rule/hook/MCP surfaces.
products.nextAction: Community PRs that improve Cursor behavior are welcome; contributors must include strict Cursor self-test evidence, especially for native choice-surface claims, and changes can merge only after the evidence passes review.

products.label: OpenCode

evidence.claim: ECC targets OpenCode through its upstream CLI installer.
ECC install help lists opencode as a supported target.

products.decision: Keep as an install target only; do not treat as a formal Meta_Kim projection.
products.nextAction: Add a formal profile and sync tests only if Meta_Kim owns an OpenCode projection.

products.label: Qwen Code

evidence.claim: ECC targets Qwen through its upstream CLI installer.
ECC install help lists qwen as a supported target.

products.decision: Keep as an ECC install target; generic runtime compatibility still needs a probe.
products.nextAction: Probe Qwen skill, agent, hook, and MCP surfaces before promotion.

products.label: Qoder CLI

evidence.claim: Open issue requests qodercli compatibility.
Qoder skills are folders containing SKILL.md under user or project .qoder/skills.
Qoder subagents use markdown files under user or project .qoder/agents.
Qoder hooks are configured in settings.json and can block via exit code 2 for supported events.
Qoder MCP servers can be configured with qodercli mcp add or .mcp.json/project settings.
ECC install help does not list qoder as a supported target.

products.decision: Admit Qoder as candidate_probe only: official docs prove compatible primitives, but Meta_Kim lacks profile/layout/sync validation and ECC currently omits qoder.
products.nextAction: Use a generic adapter probe first; promote only after adding a profile, projection layout, generated target paths, sync tests, and install policy.
